1_directory_structure_and_models.overview: Private messaging module located at apps/private_messages/ with feature-flagged E2E encryption

Conversation.description: Top-level container for direct or group conversations

ConversationParticipant.description: Represents an actor's participation in a conversation with key acknowledgment state

fields.last_read_at: DateTimeField (tracks when participant last read messages)
fields.acknowledged_remote_key_id: CharField (max 128, blank allowed) - stores remote key id participant acknowledged

ConversationParticipant.key_change_warning_contract: Fields acknowledged_remote_key_id and acknowledged_remote_key_at track when user acknowledged remote safety key

UserIdentityKey.description: Per-actor cryptographic identity keys for E2E encryption with rotation support

fields.public_key: TextField - public key material for asymmetric crypto
fields.fingerprint_hex: CharField (max 64) - SHA256 hex fingerprint for safety verification
fields.is_active: BooleanField (default True) - marks current active key
fields.rotated_at: DateTimeField (nullable) - timestamp when key was rotated out

key_lifecycle.active_marker: is_active=True supports rotation without deleting history
key_lifecycle.rotation_signaling: rotated_at field marks key transition events
key_lifecycle.registration_flow: Per-actor keys registered as UserIdentityKey objects
key_lifecycle.browser_vs_server: Browser retains private key locally; server stores only public key metadata

EncryptedMessageEnvelope.description: Encrypted message container with metadata-only storage (no plaintext messages)

fields.sender: ForeignKey to actors.Actor nullable (SET_NULL) - message author
fields.recipient_actor: ForeignKey to actors.Actor nullable (SET_NULL) - intended recipient
fields.sender_key_id: CharField (max 128) - which sender key was used
fields.recipient_key_id: CharField (max 128) - which recipient key was used
fields.aad_hash: CharField (max 255, blank) - additional authenticated data hash
fields.read_at: DateTimeField (nullable) - when recipient marked as read

constraints: UniqueConstraint on (conversation, sender, client_message_id) when client_message_id is not blank - uniq_client_message_per_sender

storage_contract.prohibited: No plaintext message field - encrypted-envelope-only storage path enforced

documentation.note: Keep disabled until PM security gate sign-off

environment_setup.lifecycle_note: Phase 5 rollout behind explicit feature gating until security review completed

core_enforcement_function.behavior: Raises exception when flag is False; prevents any PM service operation

flag_check_function.code:
def is_private_messages_enabled() -> bool:
    return bool(getattr(settings, 'FEATURE_PM_E2E_ENABLED', False))

FEATURE_PM_DEV_CIPHERTEXT_PREVIEW.purpose: Development-only ciphertext preview in conversation detail

enforcement_checklist.item_1: Before enabling in any environment, complete threat model checklist in docs/adr/0001-pm-e2e-foundation.md
enforcement_checklist.item_2: Confirm no plaintext PM fields or debug logs emitted in app/worker logs
enforcement_checklist.item_3: Run PM test suite: python manage.py test apps.private_messages
enforcement_checklist.item_4: Run system checks: python manage.py check

feature_flag_check.code:
if not is_private_messages_enabled():
    return render(request, 'private_messages/list.html', {'pm_enabled': False, 'conversations': []})

entry_points.description: List all conversations for logged-in user with optional unread filter
entry_points.action: Displays PM list or disabled message when flag is False

entry_points.description: Initiate or reuse direct conversation with another actor
entry_points.action: Creates conversation if new; reuses if exists; checks for self-DM and blocks

entry_points.description: Display conversation detail with message history and send form
entry_points.action: Shows encrypted envelopes; provides context for message sending

validation: Requires both participants have active identity keys
Declines if either recipient or sender key is missing

entry_points.description: Primary message writing entry point (client-initiated encrypted send)
entry_points.action: Stores EncryptedMessageEnvelope; publishes websocket event if enabled

underlying_service.purpose: Phase 5 bootstrap utility for local PM testing and onboarding

entry_points.description: Create or rotate local identity key (for development/onboarding)
entry_points.action: Generates bootstrap key with secrets.token_urlsafe + SHA256 fingerprint

feature_flag_check.code:
if not is_private_messages_enabled():
    return JsonResponse({'ok': False, 'error': 'Private messaging is not enabled.'}, status=400)

underlying_service.purpose: Register browser-generated public identity key; rotates previous active keys

payload_validation: key_id must be provided and non-blank
public_key must be provided and non-blank
fingerprint_hex must be 64 char lowercase hex
ValidationError returned as JSON if invalid
entry_points.atomic_rotation: Marks old active keys as is_active=False and sets rotated_at timestamp
entry_points.description: Register browser-generated public key and atomically rotate previous keys
entry_points.action: Returns JSON with created flag, key_id, fingerprint_hex

entry_points.description: Acknowledge remote participant's current safety key to clear key-change warning
entry_points.key_change_warning_contract: Updates participant-scoped acknowledgment fields to clear warning UI

entry_points.feature_flag_check: Check performed in underlying service functions
entry_points.purpose: Poll endpoint for new envelopes after cursor (pagination via envelope timestamp + id)
entry_points.description: Fetch new messages since last cursor with cursor-based pagination

3_entry_points_for_pm_message_writing.entry_points_summary: 7 primary entry points for PM interaction; all check is_private_messages_enabled() or redirect/error when flag is False

tests.code:
with self.assertRaises(ImproperlyConfigured):
    create_direct_conversation(
        created_by=self.alice.actor,
        participant_a=self.alice.actor,
        participant_b=self.bob.actor,
    )
tests.verifies: Service-layer blocks conversation creation when FEATURE_PM_E2E_ENABLED=False via require_private_messages_enabled()

tests.code:
conversation = Conversation.objects.create(created_by=self.alice.actor)
with self.assertRaises(ImproperlyConfigured):
    store_encrypted_message(
        conversation=conversation,
        sender=self.alice.actor,
        recipient_actor=self.bob.actor,
        ciphertext='base64:ciphertext',
        message_nonce='base64:nonce',
        sender_key_id='alice-key-1',
        recipient_key_id='bob-key-1',
    )
tests.verifies: Message storage blocked when feature disabled; requires feature gate for envelope persistence

coverage_assessment.service_layer_gates: ✓ Covered - create_direct_conversation and store_encrypted_message both tested
coverage_assessment.view_layer_gates: Partial - View-layer redirects tested via PrivateMessagesHtmlFlowTests with enabled flag
coverage_assessment.critical_gaps: No explicit test for disabled-flag view responses; only tests with flag enabled

PrivateMessagesHtmlFlowTests.focus: End-to-end HTML view rendering and user interaction flows; key change warning tests

5_key_rotation_and_identity_key_manageme.overview: Two-phase key management: server bootstrap (development) and browser registration (production)

fields_for_rotation.is_active: Boolean flag marks current active key (only one per actor active at a time)
fields_for_rotation.rotated_at: DateTimeField records when key was rotated out

ensure_active_identity_key.signature: def ensure_active_identity_key(*, actor, rotate: bool = False) -> tuple[UserIdentityKey, bool]:
ensure_active_identity_key.purpose: Create local active identity key when missing, or rotate when requested (Phase 5 bootstrap utility)
ensure_active_identity_key.behavior_no_rotation: Returns existing active key without creating new one

behavior_with_rotation.step_1: Marks all existing is_active=True keys as is_active=False

register_browser_identity_key.signature: def register_browser_identity_key(*, actor, key_id: str, public_key: str, fingerprint_hex: str) -> tuple[UserIdentityKey, bool]:
register_browser_identity_key.purpose: Register browser-generated public identity key and atomically rotate previous active keys
register_browser_identity_key.design: Browser retains private key locally; server stores only public key metadata
register_browser_identity_key.atomic_transaction: Wrapped in transaction.atomic() for rotation safety
validation: key_id must be non-blank after strip()
public_key must be non-blank after strip()
Raises ValidationError if any validation fails
register_browser_identity_key.conflict_detection: Raises ValidationError if key_id already registered to different actor

if_key_exists_but_inactive.action: Marks all other is_active=True keys as is_active=False with rotated_at timestamp
if_key_exists_but_inactive.then: Reactivates existing key by setting is_active=True, rotated_at=None

if_key_doesnt_exist.action: Marks all existing is_active=True keys as is_active=False with rotated_at timestamp

safety_fingerprint_function.contract: Return deterministic 64-char hex safety fingerprint for two participants
safety_fingerprint_function.determinism: Order-invariant - same result regardless of parameter order

algorithm.step_1: Normalize both fingerprints: lowercase and remove whitespace

identicon_seed_function.signature: def compute_identicon_seed(local_fingerprint: str, remote_fingerprint: str) -> str:
identicon_seed_function.contract: Return deterministic seed for visual fingerprint (identicon) generation
identicon_seed_function.usage: Used in conversation detail context to render identicon for visual verification

key_change_detection.signature: def has_remote_key_changed(*, acknowledged_remote_key_id: str, remote_key_id: str) -> bool:
key_change_detection.logic: Returns True if current remote_key_id differs from acknowledged_remote_key_id
key_change_detection.implementation: Normalizes both (strip), returns bool comparison after null-safety checks
key_change_detection.usage_in_views: apps/private_messages/views.py:77-83 uses this to set key_change_warning text

key_change_warning_contract.summary: Per-conversation acknowledgment state stored on ConversationParticipant (Slice 5.3)

fields.acknowledged_remote_key_id: CharField storing which remote key id user has acknowledged

ux_flow.step_1: Views detect key change via has_remote_key_changed()
ux_flow.step_2: If changed, sets key_change_warning context and key_change_acknowledged=False
ux_flow.step_3: Template renders warning: 'has a new active safety key. Verify the fingerprint before trusting new messages.'
ux_flow.step_4: User clicks acknowledge button (POST to acknowledge-key endpoint)
ux_flow.step_6: Warning clears on next detail view (has_remote_key_changed returns False)

rotation_lifecycle.frequency: Once per actor (or when explicitly rotated)

rotation_lifecycle.creates: New key with is_active=True if new key_id; reactivates if dormant
rotation_lifecycle.rotation: Old keys marked is_active=False with rotated_at timestamp

rotation_lifecycle.mechanism: Client detects remote key_id changed; server stores acknowledged state
rotation_lifecycle.display: Key change warning shown until user acknowledges new key

rotation_lifecycle.epoch_support: key_epoch field reserved for future ratchet/session-key lifecycle
